understanding.goal_understanding: To get ANY pushable object — the selected piece or any pushable block — to reach a target x-coordinate (at or beyond level.target_x).
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is grid-based with actions MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN shifting the selected piece by 3 units in that direction (e.g., MOVE_UP from [0,0] to [0,-3]). No pushing or walls encountered in these turns; SELECT allows choosing a piece by id. Steps have a budget that decreases per action.

orient_history: 2D pixel grid (moves=3px=1cell). Controllable sel moves to targets unless blocked; pushes adjacent blocks if they can move. Walls impassable/non-pushable. Empty space elsewhere. Win by sel or block left_x >=6. Wall blocks y=0 path; bypass via y<0 or y>0 lanes.

Grid-based world with 3x3 pixel cells (1 step = 3 pixels). Controllables (sel) and blocks (pb) are pushable; walls (wt) block movement/pushing. Movement targets show intended positions; blocked if occupied by immovable or if push target blocked. Adjacent lists show touching objects for potential pushing. Goal: any controllable or block position[0] >= 6. pb at x=3 blocked right by wall at [6,0]; cannot push directly right. Open space above (negative y) allows sel to path right to x=6 unimpeded.

Grid uses pixel coords (3 pixels/cell); MOVE actions shift selected controllable by 3 units in cardinal directions unless blocked (adjacent immovable or out-of-bounds); pushable objects include selected piece ('sel') and blocks ('pb'); win by any pushable reaching x>=6; walls immovable; adjacent_ids show pushables for potential pushing on move into them.

history.summary: Turn 1: MOVE_UP | Move sel up to [0,-3] (open), then right to [3,-3]/[6,-3] (unobstructed, x>=6 wins in 3 steps total). Efficient vs pushing pb (harder, blocked).
history.observe: Initial state: Player (sel) at [0,0] size 3x3, block (pb) at [3,0] size 3x3 adjacent right, wall (wt) at [6,0] size 3x3. Right blocked due to pb unable to move into wt. Up/down/left open. Target_x=6 for sel or pb x-position.
history.orient: 2D pixel grid (moves=3px=1cell). Controllable sel moves to targets unless blocked; pushes adjacent blocks if they can move. Walls impassable/non-pushable. Empty space elsewhere. Win by sel or block left_x >=6. Wall blocks y=0 path; bypass via y<0 or y>0 lanes.
history.decide: Move sel up to [0,-3] (open), then right to [3,-3]/[6,-3] (unobstructed, x>=6 wins in 3 steps total). Efficient vs pushing pb (harder, blocked).

history.summary: Turn 2: MOVE_RIGHT | MOVE_RIGHT to [3,-3], then next MOVE_RIGHT to [6,-3] for sel.x=6 >= target_x=6 to win. Efficient 2-move path; avoids blocked pb push.
history.observe: Selected piece moved up from [0,0] to [0,-3]. No other objects moved. Steps used: 1, remaining: 9.
history.orient: Grid-based world with 3x3 pixel cells (1 step = 3 pixels). Controllables (sel) and blocks (pb) are pushable; walls (wt) block movement/pushing. Movement targets show intended positions; blocked if occupied by immovable or if push target blocked. Adjacent lists show touching objects for potential pushing. Goal: any controllable or block position[0] >= 6. pb at x=3 blocked right by wall at [6,0]; cannot push directly right. Open space above (negative y) allows sel to path right to x=6 unimpeded.
history.decide: MOVE_RIGHT to [3,-3], then next MOVE_RIGHT to [6,-3] for sel.x=6 >= target_x=6 to win. Efficient 2-move path; avoids blocked pb push.

history.summary: Turn 3: MOVE_RIGHT | MOVE_RIGHT to shift 'sel' to [6, -3], achieving x=6 >= target_x=6 for immediate win; right_blocked=false confirms clear path; no need to push 'pb' or select others.
history.observe: Since last turn (MOVE_RIGHT), selected piece advanced from [0, -3] to [3, -3] as expected, no blocks or unexpected changes; down now adjacent to pushable block 'pb' at [3, 0], right path clear to [6, -3].

history.decide: MOVE_RIGHT to shift 'sel' to [6, -3], achieving x=6 >= target_x=6 for immediate win; right_blocked=false confirms clear path; no need to push 'pb' or select others.
